violations.text: (Various food product) was observed held at (48-53) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling.PIC Voluntarily disposed of food product Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026

Unpasteurized eggs were observed in the refrigerator. Per interview, unpasteurized eggs are used when cooking scrambled eggs for young children. PIC informed to use unpasteurized eggs for baking and obtain pasteurized eggs for making eggs for young children. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/17/2025

Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. PIC labeled bottle during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/17/2025

The concentration of the spray bottle sanitizer solution was greater than 200 parts per million (ppm). The required concentration for chlorine sanitizer is between 50-100 ppm. PIC refilled bottle. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/17/2025

(Various food product) was observed held at (52-58) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC voluntarily disposed of food product. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was (0) parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. New Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2024

Thermometers are lacking in the (all) refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the (chlorine ) sanitizing solution for the (3 bay sink and dishwasher ). Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2024
Cases of water were observed stored on the floor in the staff lounge/conference room. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2024

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dish machine. Inspector provided small supply of test strips during inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was zero parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Staff will utilize 3-bay sink for sanitizing utensils until dishwasher repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 11/22/2023
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dish washer and 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 11/08/2023
